name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا تَدَايَنتُم بِدَيْنٍ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى فَٱكْتُبُوهُ ۚ وَلْيَكْتُب بَّيْنَكُمْ كَاتِبٌۢ بِٱلْعَدْلِ ۚ وَلَا يَأْبَ كَاتِبٌ أَن يَكْتُبَ كَمَا عَلَّمَهُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ فَلْيَكْتُبْ وَلْيُمْلِلِ ٱلَّذِى عَلَيْهِ ٱلْحَقُّ وَلْيَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ رَبَّهُۥ وَلَا يَبْخَسْ مِنْهُ شَيْـًٔا ۚ فَإِن كَانَ ٱلَّذِى عَلَيْهِ ٱلْحَقُّ سَفِيهًا أَوْ ضَعِيفًا أَوْ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَن يُمِلَّ هُوَ فَلْيُمْلِلْ وَلِيُّهُۥ بِٱلْعَدْلِ ۚ وَٱسْتَشْهِدُوا۟ شَهِيدَيْنِ مِن رِّجَالِكُمْ ۖ فَإِن لَّمْ يَكُونَا رَجُلَيْنِ فَرَجُلٌ وَٱمْرَأَتَانِ مِمَّن تَرْضَوْنَ مِنَ ٱلشُّهَدَآءِ أَن تَضِلَّ إِحْدَىٰهُمَا فَتُذَكِّرَ إِحْدَىٰهُمَا ٱلْأُخْرَىٰ ۚ وَلَا يَأْبَ ٱلشُّهَدَآءُ إِذَا مَا دُعُوا۟ ۚ وَلَا تَسْـَٔمُوٓا۟ أَن تَكْتُبُوهُ صَغِيرًا أَوْ كَبِيرًا إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلِهِۦ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَقْسَطُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَأَقْوَمُ لِلشَّهَـٰدَةِ وَأَدْنَىٰٓ أَلَّا تَرْتَابُوٓا۟ ۖ إِلَّآ أَن تَكُونَ تِجَـٰرَةً حَاضِرَةً تُدِيرُونَهَا بَيْنَكُمْ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَلَّا تَكْتُبُوهَا ۗ وَأَشْهِدُوٓا۟ إِذَا تَبَايَعْتُمْ ۚ وَلَا يُضَآرَّ كَاتِبٌ وَلَا شَهِيدٌ ۚ وَإِن تَفْعَلُوا۟ فَإِنَّهُۥ فُسُوقٌۢ بِكُمْ ۗ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۖ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (٢٨٢)
```

```english
O you who have faith! When you contract a loan for a specified term, write it down. Let a writer write with honesty between you, and let not the writer refuse to write as Allah has taught him. So let him write, and let the one who incurs the debt dictate, and let him be wary of Allah, his Lord, and not diminish anything from it. But if the debtor be feeble-minded, or weak, or incapable of dictating himself, then let his guardian dictate with honesty, and take as witness two witnesses from your men, and if there are not two men, then a man and two women—from those whom you approve as witnesses—so that if one of the two defaults the other will remind her. The witnesses must not refuse when they are called, and do not consider it wearisome to write it down, whether it be a big or small sum, [as a loan lent] until its term. That is more just with Allah and more upright in respect to testimony, and the likeliest way to avoid doubt, unless it is an on-the-spot deal you transact between yourselves, in which case there is no sin upon you not to write it. Take witnesses when you make a deal, and let no harm be done to the writer or witness, and if you did that, it would be sinful of you. Be wary of Allah and Allah will teach you, and Allah has knowledge of all things. (282)
```

```arabic
۞ وَإِن كُنتُمْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ وَلَمْ تَجِدُوا۟ كَاتِبًا فَرِهَـٰنٌ مَّقْبُوضَةٌ ۖ فَإِنْ أَمِنَ بَعْضُكُم بَعْضًا فَلْيُؤَدِّ ٱلَّذِى ٱؤْتُمِنَ أَمَـٰنَتَهُۥ وَلْيَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ رَبَّهُۥ ۗ وَلَا تَكْتُمُوا۟ ٱلشَّهَـٰدَةَ ۚ وَمَن يَكْتُمْهَا فَإِنَّهُۥٓ ءَاثِمٌ قَلْبُهُۥ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ عَلِيمٌ (٢٨٣)
```

```english
If you are on a journey and cannot find a writer, then a retained pledge [shall suffice]. And if one of you entrusts [an asset] to another, let him who is trusted deliver his trust, and let him be wary of Allah, his Lord. And do not conceal your testimony; anyone who conceals it, his heart will indeed be sinful, and Allah knows well what you do. (283)
```

## (Commentary)

**His saying, the Exalted:** إِذا تداينتم (When you contract a debt) etc. Mutual debt (التداين) refers to the act of people transacting credit with one another. Dictation (الإملال) and (الإملاء) both refer to a person reciting to a scribe what is to be written. Defrauding (البخس) is to diminish or act unjustly. Weariness (السأمة) is boredom or fatigue. Harming (المضارة) is a reciprocal form (مفاعلة) derived from harm (الضرر), used for actions between two parties or otherwise. Transgression (الفسوق) is the departure from obedience (الطاعة). Pledges (الرهان)—also recited as (رُهُن) with two *dammahs*—are both plurals of pledge (الرهن), meaning the mortgaged property (المرهون).

The use of an explicit noun where a pronoun would suffice in His saying, the Exalted: **فإِن كان الذي عليه الحق** (but if he who owes the right), is to remove ambiguity that would arise if the pronoun referred back to the previously mentioned scribe.

The prominent pronoun in His saying: **أن يمل هو فليملل وليه** (to dictate, then let his guardian dictate), serves the purpose of associating the one who owes the right with his guardian. This case differs from the first two cases in that the guardian in the first two is the one solely responsible for the command. In contrast, in this case, the one who owes the right participates with the guardian in the action. It is as if it were said: whatever part of the task he is capable of, he must do, and what he is incapable of, his guardian must do.

His saying: **أن تضل إِحديهما** (lest one of them should err), is based on the implied meaning of "for fear that one of them should err." In His saying: **إِحديهما الاخرى** (one of them... the other), an explicit noun is placed where a pronoun could be. The subtle point (النكتة) here is the difference in the meaning of the word in the two instances: the first refers to either one of them indefinitely, while the second refers to the one remaining after the other has erred. Thus, the two meanings are distinct.

His saying: **واتقوا** (And be God-conscious), is a command for godwariness (التقوى) — *the state of being consciously protective against disobeying Allah* — regarding the commands and prohibitions Allah has set forth for them in this verse. As for His saying: **ويعلمكم الله والله بكل شيء عليم** (and Allah teaches you, and Allah has knowledge of all things), it is a newly commenced sentence (كلام مستأنف) intended to express divine favor (الامتنان), similar to His saying, the Exalted, in the verse of inheritance: **يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ أَن تَضِلُّوا** — **"Allah clarifies for you lest you should go astray"** (An-Nisa: 176). Thus, the intent is to express favor through the teaching of the laws of religion (شرائع الدين) and matters of the lawful (الحلال) and the prohibited (الحرام).

Regarding what has been said—that His saying: **واتقوا الله ويعلمكم الله** (And be God-conscious of Allah, and Allah teaches you) indicates that godwariness (التقوى) is a cause for divine teaching—it must be noted that while this is a truth supported by the Book and the Sunnah, this particular verse does not indicate it due to the presence of the coordinating conjunction "and" (واو العطف). Furthermore, such a meaning does not suit the context (سياق) of the verse or the connection of its conclusion to its beginning.

What we have mentioned is supported by the repetition of the Majestic Name (لفظ الجلالة) a second time. Had His saying **ويعلمكم الله** (and Allah teaches you) not been a newly commenced sentence, the context would have required saying "He teaches you" (يعلمكم) by using a hidden pronoun for the subject. In His saying, the Exalted: **واتقوا الله ويعلمكم الله ان الله بكل شيء عليم** (And be God-conscious of Allah, and Allah teaches you, and indeed Allah has knowledge of all things), the Name was made explicit the first and second time because they occur in two independent sentences, and it was made explicit a third time to indicate causality (التعليل), as if it were said: He has knowledge of all things because He is Allah.

Know that these two verses indicate approximately twenty rulings from the fundamental laws of religion (أُصول أحكام الدين), pledges (الرهن), and others. The narrations (الاخبار) regarding them and related matters are numerous, but their investigation belongs to jurisprudence (الفقه). For this reason, we have chosen to overlook them; whoever desires to investigate them should consult the appropriate sources in jurisprudence.
